details: Starting eltwise migration run, target: 87 more kernels

Reg A/C fixes did NOT change Type-1/Type-2 classification rubric. Reg A made BinaryFpu Bcast safe (was already Type-1 candidate). Reg C made non-clash chains correctly reconfig pack (was already Type-1 mechanically). fp32 annotation is methodology. Net headroom likely zero new Type-1 promotions — all 37 v1 Type-1 blocks already migrated.

v1 audit Type-1 set (37 blocks) fully migrated. Discovered new Type-1 candidate: moreh_adamw L216-225 (cb_tmp1 = cb_one - cb_scalar_args[beta2_tile]). Also v1 misclassified asymmetric-Pinned-index pattern (cb_yydysum = cb_y[wt] * cb_ydysum[0]) — these ARE Type-1 with Q4 single-Pinned mode + per-element a_tile_idx/b_tile_idx member fields. No Reg A/C-driven Type-2 to Type-1 promotion of distinct classes (mask/held-cb/multi-dest/macro-inject still blockers).

partial_kernel_audit_v2.md written. Net headroom: 3 newly-Type-1 blocks across 2 kernels (T1.5-01 moreh_adamw L216-225, T1.5-02/03 moreh_layer_norm_backward_input_grad_small). Verdict: Type-1.5 NOT worth standalone; go to Type-2 MaskInject (17 kernels) + HeldCbBinaryFpu (16 kernels) extension pass.
